standard_variable.semantic_grain: one row per positive state episode

database_mapping.source_grain: one official ventilation status interval row
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained positive tracheostomy status state episode row

identifier_normalization.subject_id_role: patient-level identifier from trusted stay map
identifier_normalization.hadm_id_role: hospital-admission-level identifier from trusted stay map
identifier_normalization.stay_id_role: ICU-stay-level identifier from official ventilation source and trusted stay map

adjacent_status_boundary.boundary_reason: This same-name variable represents tracheostomy status only. It must not be auto-equated to invasive ventilation or any broader respiratory-support composite.

state_episode_build_translation.positive_state_translation: retain official ventilation rows whose ventilation_status is exactly Tracheostomy
state_episode_build_translation.continuity_translation: keep official Tracheostomy interval boundaries; do not union invasive ventilation, noninvasive ventilation, HFNC, or supplemental oxygen rows into this asset
state_episode_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no positive Tracheostomy episode under this source-status rule

validation_contract.positive_only_no_row_policy_check: false rows are not emitted; absence of row is not universal proof of no tracheostomy status
notes: The retained source is official source_supplied_derived.ventilation with ventilation_status=Tracheostomy.
InvasiveVent, NonInvasiveVent, HFNC, and SupplementalOxygen are adjacent statuses but are excluded from this same-name output.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation until a more fully spec-native runner replaces it.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_tracheostomy_status_active outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path rather than side SQL or ad hoc rebuilds.
